name: Loanstar Title Loans
description_short: Loanstar Title Loans Carrollton, Texas — LoanStar Title Loans offers car title loans up to $10,000 at multiple Fort Worth, TX locations. Founded in 1990...
description_long:
LoanStar Title Loans was founded in 1990 in Jonesboro, GA, making it one of the longer-tenured title lending companies in the United States. The company describes its mission as helping hardworking Americans leverage the equity in their vehicles to access needed cash. In the Fort Worth area alone, LoanStar operates multiple branches — including locations on Camp Bowie, McCart Ave, E. Lancaster Ave — as well as nearby outlets in Haltom City, Richland Hills, and Burleson, indicating a significant regional footprint across the Dallas-Fort Worth metro.

LoanStar's core product is the auto title loan, also marketed as a car title loan. Loans are available up to $10,000, and the company offers both single installment and multiple installment loan structures. The application process is designed to be simple: applicants need three things — their vehicle, a government-issued photo ID, and a clear vehicle title with no existing liens. The 8304 Camp Bowie Fort Worth location accepts repayment by cash, check, debit card, or Western Union, and an online payment portal is available for added convenience. A toll-free line (877-511-CASH) is accessible for inquiries.

Several features distinguish LoanStar from fly-by-night title lenders. The company is licensed and regulated by the Texas Office of Consumer Credit Commissioner (OCCC), and publicly posts fee schedules on its website for both single and multiple installment loans at the $600, $1,200, and $2,000 levels — a degree of fee transparency required by Texas law. The website offers a Spanish-language option, reflecting outreach to the bilingual Fort Worth community. Saturday hours (9AM–2PM) extend accessibility beyond the standard weekday window, and the company's 35-plus years of operation suggests institutional stability.

Title loans are high-cost credit products, and LoanStar is no exception. While the OCCC license and posted fee schedules provide regulatory accountability, borrowers must carefully review the effective APR before committing — title loan fees in Texas can translate to triple-digit annualized rates. The defining risk is collateral: if a borrower cannot repay, LoanStar holds the legal right to repossess and sell the vehicle. For a car-owning consumer facing a genuine cash emergency who has exhausted lower-cost alternatives, LoanStar offers a regulated and accessible option, but the vehicle risk makes it a product of last resort rather than a routine borrowing tool.
address: 2000 E Belt Line Rd, Carrollton, TX 75006

company_info.headquarters: 2000 E Belt Line Rd, Carrollton, TX 75006

pros: Loans available up to $10,000, higher ceiling than typical payday or emergency cash lenders
Founded in 1990 — over 35 years of operational history provides institutional stability
Licensed by Texas OCCC with publicly posted fee schedules for $600, $1,200, and $2,000 loan amounts
Multiple payment methods accepted: cash, check, debit card, and Western Union
Saturday hours (9AM–2PM) available, unlike many traditional lenders
Simple, three-item application requirement: vehicle, photo ID, and clear title
Online payment portal available in addition to in-store payment
Spanish-language website option for bilingual customers
Multiple Fort Worth-area locations provide convenient access across the metro
cons: Vehicle serves as collateral — failure to repay can result in repossession of the borrower's car
Title loans in Texas frequently carry triple-digit APRs; fee schedules are posted but prominent APR disclosures are not highlighted on location pages
Weekday hours limited to 10AM–6PM, narrower window than banks or online lenders
Clear title required — borrowers with any existing lien on their vehicle do not qualify
No mention of credit counseling or hardship programs if a borrower cannot repay
best_for: Vehicle owners with a clear car title who need fast cash and do not qualify for traditional personal loans
Consumers with poor or no credit history who have been turned down elsewhere
Fort Worth or DFW-area residents who prefer in-person loan transactions over online-only lenders
Borrowers needing more than $1,000 but who lack the credit profile for an unsecured personal loan
diagnosis: LoanStar Title Loans is best suited for DFW-area consumers who own their vehicle outright, need cash quickly (up to $10,000), and cannot qualify for lower-cost credit options. The main caveat is the collateral risk inherent in all title loans — borrowers who miss payments risk losing their vehicle, making this product appropriate only for genuine emergencies after exhausting alternatives like credit union PALs, personal loans, or family assistance.
services: Car title loans (auto title loans) up to $10,000
In-store payment acceptance (cash, check, debit card, Western Union)
Multiple DFW-area branch locations for in-person service
